verses.text: David says in his heart, 'I shall now perish one day by the hand of Saul,' and flees to the Philistines. After all his deliverances, fear whispers that God will fail him at last. Even strong faith has its low hours, when the heart forgets the help it has so often seen.

David says in his heart, 'I shall now perish one day by the hand of Saul,' and flees to the Philistines. After all his deliverances, fear whispers that God will fail him at last. Even strong faith has its low hours, when the heart forgets the help it has so often seen.

David says in his heart, 'I shall now perish one day by the hand of Saul,' and flees to the Philistines. After all his deliverances, fear whispers that God will fail him at last. Even strong faith has its low hours, when the heart forgets the help it has so often seen.

David says in his heart, 'I shall now perish one day by the hand of Saul,' and flees to the Philistines. After all his deliverances, fear whispers that God will fail him at last. Even strong faith has its low hours, when the heart forgets the help it has so often seen.

David is given Ziklag and settles among the Philistines for sixteen months. To escape Saul he takes refuge with God's enemies — safer, but no place for a man of God to dwell. Fear can drive us to compromises that buy us peace at the cost of our witness.

David is given Ziklag and settles among the Philistines for sixteen months. To escape Saul he takes refuge with God's enemies — safer, but no place for a man of God to dwell. Fear can drive us to compromises that buy us peace at the cost of our witness.

David is given Ziklag and settles among the Philistines for sixteen months. To escape Saul he takes refuge with God's enemies — safer, but no place for a man of God to dwell. Fear can drive us to compromises that buy us peace at the cost of our witness.

From Ziklag David raids old enemies of Israel, leaving no one alive to tell. His sword still serves God's purposes, yet his life here is tangled with deceit and bloodshed. Living among the world's people, he is slowly drawn into the world's shadows.

From Ziklag David raids old enemies of Israel, leaving no one alive to tell. His sword still serves God's purposes, yet his life here is tangled with deceit and bloodshed. Living among the world's people, he is slowly drawn into the world's shadows.

David lies to Achish about whom he has raided, and Achish comes to trust him completely, thinking David has made himself hateful to Israel. The compromise deepens; one deception breeds another. A foot in the enemy's camp soon ties the tongue to falsehood.
